title: Harte Secur
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{City |image=[[File:Harte_Secur_take_2.png]] |name=Harte Secur |constructed= |destroyed=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' |rebuilt= |builder= |mayor= |celestialbody=[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] |continent= |location= |climate= |interest= |population= |demonym= |affiliation=[[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Royal House of Naboo]] }} {{Quote|Your army is online. Destroy Harte Secur!|Nute Gunray to OOM-9|audio=ArmyOnline.flac|}} '''Harte Secur''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] that lay on a peninsula south of [[Lake Vis]]. Its layout consisted of a central courtyard with a [[command center]], with a paved outer area containing various civilian and military buildings. Harte Secur was also defended by [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] and [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]]. [[Harte Secur Airbase]] was located southwest of the city, and it was known for training some of Naboo's best [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]]. A [[Research Center]] and an [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Shipyard (water)|shipyard]] were also attached to the city externally. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], a [[Droid control program]] used to keep the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] active was stolen by a [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]] [[Force Command Two|team]] and taken to the Harte Secur research center. As the Naboo were able to slice the program and deactivate a portion of the Droid Army, [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] assigned the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] with a task force to retrieve the program. After storming the research center, a [[Unidentified Neimoidian technician (Harte Secur)|technician]] reactivated a [[Deactivated Camp|camp]] of deactivated battle [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. OOM-9 was ordered to use the new forces to destroy Harte Secur, overcoming its defenders, including troopers, [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]], and [[Mech/Legends|mechs]]. The Trade Federation then occupied Harte Secur, building a new [[command center]] atop its ruins. Later, the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] began preparing to take on the Trade Federation in the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], for which they needed [[Fambaa/Legends|fambaa]] beasts, which were held in [[Camp Six]] near Harte Secur. The Gungan [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] had the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]] attack Camp Six and subsequently had them [[Liberation of Harte Secur|liberate]] Harte Secur, in order to draw the Trade Federation out of Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. ==Description== ===Naboo city=== ====Overview and northwestern side==== {{Quote|But we must retrieve the droid control program from the stronghold of Harte Secur.|Nute Gunray to OOM-9|audio=ProgramRetrieve.flac|}} [[File:Naboo_FFGRebellion.png|thumb|right|250px|Naboo, Harte Secur's location]] Harte Secur was a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], inhabited by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] and loyal to the [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Royal House of Naboo]]. It was located on a peninsula south of [[Lake Vis]] with various [[Tree/Legends|treelines]] on its coast. Most of Harte Secur's surface was paved with bricks, and the architecture was similar in style to Naboo's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. Three stone pathways led to the city from the south, and each was guarded by a pair of [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] and a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]]. Between the western and central entrance there were three houses, a gazebo, and a [[Power core/Legends|power core]]. The northwestern side of the city had buildings on the opposite sides of its path. The westernmost side had a tower, six houses, another power core, and a [[Mech/Legends|mech]] [[Factory/Legends|factory]]. The corner of that side of the city had a power core, a power station, and a troop center, as well as a temple and a house on a small [[Grass/Legends|grass]]-covered hill where [[Flower/Legends|flowers]] grew. The eastern side of that area of Harte Secur had a prefab shelter, a [[War Center]] with a power core and a turret next to it, two prefab shelters next to a heavy weapons factory, another power core, a troop center, a [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]], another power core and a [[Research Center]]. ====Center and eastern areas==== [[File:Harte_Secur_shipyard.png|thumb|left|350px|The shipyard outside of Harte Secur]] The center of Harte Secur was surrounded by walls in a rectangular formation, with a large archway leading into it. Inside it was a body of water surrounded by a fenced path. A total of six obelisks were placed in an even formation on both sides of the water. A [[command center]] was built at the top of the central courtyard of the city, with two turrets and two power cores on its sides. The area north of the command center was covered with trees. The eastern side of the city had three houses, a power core, two different types of turrets, and a mech factory, all on a paved area that ended as the ground to the north was elevated. The elevated area had a variety of trees and flowers, as well as a power core, and at its flat peak was another paved built up area. That area had, in clockwise order, a turret, two prefab shelters, another turret, a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], a prefab shelter, two houses, and an [[Airbase/Legends|airbase]]. ====External sites==== [[File:Harte_Secur_Research_Center.png|thumb|right|350px|The research base south of Harte Secur]] Multiple facilities associated with Harte Secur were located south of the city grounds. Directly south of the city was a base that contained a research center, two turrets, and a power core. The base was on a paved path and was surrounded by a light wall, with a gate on its eastern side. The gate was on a pathway that led northwards, eventually splitting off into the eastern and central entrances to Harte Secur. The river that surrounded Harte Secur flowed south on the western side of the city, surrounding the research base before flowing to the east. On the path to the western entrance to city there was a small [[Fish/Legends|fishing]] facility consisting of two [[Shipyard (water)|shipyards]], an [[animal nursery]], a turret, a [[Sentry tower/Legends|sentry post]], and a power core. Five [[utility trawler]]s gathered fish from the river there. The western path out of the city led down south to the [[Harte Secur Airbase]], run by the [[Naboo Airforce]]. Several of Naboo's best [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] resided there. In the forest to the east of the city there was a hidden base belonging to the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] indigenous to Naboo. ===Trade Federation occupation=== {{Quote|Boss, we spotted a town calle Harte Secur, to the south. Tis filled with many Gungan and Naboo prisoners!|[[Roos Tarpals/Legends|Roos Tarpals]] to Boss Nass|}} Following the [[Capture of Harte Secur|attack]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], Harte Secur was occupied by the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]]. ==History== ===Capture=== {{Quote|Harte Secur is neutralized.''"
"''Wonderful!|OOM-9 and Nute Gunray|audio=HarteSecurNeutralized.flac|}} [[File:Harte_Secur_destruction.png|thumb|left|300px|The capture of Harte Secur]] In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Naboo was invaded by the Trade Federation, and Harte Secur was fortified to prepare for a potential attack. The Trade Federation Droid Army established a [[Deactivated Camp|camp]] directly south of Harte Secur. Early in the invasion, the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] team [[Force Command Two]] successfully captured a [[droid control program]] from the Trade Federation [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[OOM-14/Legends|OOM-14]]. It was taken to the Harte Secur research base, where the program was used to deactivate OOM-14's forces at the nearby camp. Wary that the program could be duplicated and used to turn the Droid Army against [[Gender/Legends|him]], [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] tasked the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] with retrieving the droid control program. OOM-9 was first sent to the Deactivated Camp, where he rendezvoused with a task force sent to retrieve the program, which included five [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]], a [[bio cannon]], and a [[Unidentified Neimoidian technician (Harte Secur)|Neimoidian technician]]. The task force marched towards the research base using the eastern path, Fighting groups of [[Security Guard/Legends|royal troopers]] from Harte Secur that guarded the path and using the [[Cannon/Legends|cannon]] to destroy several turrets. At the research center, the droids [[Death/Legends|killed]] the five troopers and seven [[Elite Royal Crusader]]s protecting it and retrieved the program. After reactivating it, OOM-9 directed his expanded army towards Harte Secur on Gunray's orders, taking one of the entrances defended by two turrets. OOM-9 sent his droidekas two gun down the nearby troopers while the cannon destroyed the turrets. The bio cannon then rolled into the courtyard to destroy the command center, while the battle droids protected it by clogging the archway entrance. Harte Secur's defenders,[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] which included sixteen troopers, seven [[Royal crusader]]s, four [[Steadfast (tank)|steadfast]] tanks, four [[Champion (vehicle)|champion]] mechs, two heavy champions, three [[Naboo energy catapult]]s, four [[V-19 landspeeder]]s, and two advanced [[Tuskcat Trooper]]s, streamed in to save their city, but they were unable to stop the Trade Federation from destroying the command center and taking Harte Secur. ===Occupation and liberation=== {{Quote|Hmm… perhaps wesa should show the machineeks a piece of the Gungan Grand Army. Then theysa will be quicker to come out of Theed to meet us in a battle.|Boss Nass|}} The Trade Federation continued to occupy the ruins of Harte Secur during their invasion, establishing Camp Six nearby to hold [[Fambaa/Legends|fambaas]], creatures native to Naboo that the Trade Federation planned to skin and sell. When the Trade Federation turned the attentions towards quashing the Gungans, a [[Unidentified mariner|mariner]] captured at Harte Secur told OOM-9{{StoryCite|story=End Game|book=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|bformatted=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, 2012 edition}} about [[Ganne]], a Gungan [[Boss/Legends|boss]] of the town [[Rellias]], which ultimately led to an [[Siege of Otoh Gunga|attack]] on the Gungans. Later, the Gungan leader, Boss [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]], organized the [[Liberation of Harte Secur]] to regain the use of fambaas for the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]] in the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]. ==Behind the scenes== Harte Secur was first mentioned in the [[Prima Games]]' [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]]—released on [[November 7]], [[2001]]{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Galactic-Battlegrounds-Official/dp/0761537503|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}}—for the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', released on [[November 11]], 2001.{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}} In the game, it appears as an objective for the player to attack in "Behind the Lines," the second level of the Trade Federation campaign, and "The Liberation of Harte Secur," the sixth level of the Gungan campaign. The portrayal of Harte Secur in "Behind the Lines" differs in the strategy guide and the game. The strategy guide describes the central courtyard as located outside Harte Secur, with the command center located to the north of it. The guide still instructs the player to send their canon into the courtyard, as it can still fire at the command center from there. In the game the command center is located inside the courtyard. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Harte Secur}} *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' {{1st}} *{{StoryCite|story=End Game|book=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|bformatted=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, 2012 edition}} {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'' ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Harte Secur |es=Harte Secur }} [[Category:Cities on Naboo]] [[Category:Destroyed cities]] [[Category:Trade Federation cities]]
